meta.transcripts_note: Story language matches privacy-safe STAR summaries in Kelly_Peterson_Resume_Portfolio.docx (no raw transcripts in repo).
flags: [NEEDS CONFIRMATION: professional email for resume header and web contact]

summary: TK–8 Education Specialist (SDC + RSP/Inclusion), 26+ years across public and private schools; Clear Mild/Moderate credential and M.A. Special Education (Chapman, 2018).

Chapman Field Supervisor: formal evaluator for Special Education and Multiple Subject candidates (CalTPA + California’s 13 TPEs) with program completion recommendation authority, including documented approval and non-approval decisions.
signature_impact: Chapman evaluator: CalTPA + 13 TPEs evidence; recommends completion via approval or non-approval.
OUSD Olive: concurrent SDC + RSP/Inclusion ties IEP goals and co-teaching to bell-to-bell reality.
Mission Hills English: two-year move from avoidance to engagement; later novel electives—student-cited turning point (privacy-safe).
Chino Anna Borba: two high-risk-profile students—trust rebuilt; families back at school; formative student reflections (privacy-safe).
CUSD Riley Elementary volunteer: Revolutionary War walk-through at scale; reading data drove fifth-grade and SPED intervention cycles.
Chino grades 1–6: Master Teacher, RTI, SELPA behavior team, science fair, SSC, textbook adoption—cross-site scope.
IEP documents, classroom evidence, and district assessments feed one adjustment loop—not calendar drift.
Chapman observe–conference–document rhythm turns standards into same-week teaching adjustments in partner schools.

experience.title: Education Specialist (SDC + RSP/Inclusion)
bullets: Olive Elementary: SDC + RSP/Inclusion programming keeps models, minutes, and measurable goals aligned across formats.
MTSS/RTI + UDL: progress data drives timely scaffold shifts; preserves grade-level access when evidence supports it.
Gen-ed co-planning lands inclusion in live lessons—tightens IEP-to-classroom execution.
Family communication rhythms on goals, behaviors, and IEP windows surface momentum before meetings.
Transition routines and para coverage stabilize participation and protect core instructional minutes.
Represents caseload students when site or district decisions reset services, placements, or trajectories.

experience.title: Field Supervisor, Special Education & Multiple Subject Credential Candidates
bullets: Serves as university field supervisor for Special Education and Multiple Subject candidates, holding formal evaluation authority alongside structured coaching across clinical practice and student teaching.
Anchors written and conference feedback to California’s 13 TPEs and CalTPA milestones—lesson design, differentiation, classroom management, assessment, and portfolio requirements.
Conducts formal observations with pre- and post-conferences; records formative and summative judgments with evidence and specific next steps candidates can implement immediately.
Coordinates with mentor teachers and program leadership on concerns, documentation quality, and placement fitness while upholding confidentiality and ethical practice.
Participates in program evaluation meetings where readiness for added responsibility and milestone completion are decided.
Submits program-level readiness recommendations for credential advancement and completion, including approval and non-approval outcomes tied to documented performance.

experience.title: Special Education Instructional Aide
bullets: Delivered small-group and one-to-one support for students with disabilities under the education specialist’s direction, keeping students on IEP-aligned tasks and classroom routines.
Handled materials, transitions, and assigned supervision during structured non-instructional periods so instruction could stay focused.

experience.title: Volunteer — Fifth-Grade Classroom & Special Education Support
bullets: Volunteered in a fifth-grade classroom during a career transition, reinforcing instruction and daily routines alongside the classroom teacher.
Planned and led a schoolwide fifth-grade Revolutionary War walk-through, coordinating stations, materials, and rotations for the full grade level.
Ran weekly fifth-grade reading assessments and supported Special Education department reading assessments so teams could adjust interventions and maintain clear records.

experience.title: Junior High English Teacher
bullets: Taught junior high English with schoolwide curriculum responsibilities and individualized academic plans for students with elevated learning needs.
Partnered with the school psychologist on individualized programming spanning elementary and junior high levels.
Sponsored regional speech, spelling, and creative writing participation through ACSI-related programs.

Reluctant reader outcome (privacy-safe): supported a multi-year shift from reading avoidance to engagement; the student later chose high school novel-study electives and credited the earlier confidence foundation.
story_bullets: Reluctant reader outcome (privacy-safe): supported a multi-year shift from reading avoidance to engagement; the student later chose high school novel-study electives and credited the earlier confidence foundation.

experience.title: Elementary Teacher (grades 1–6); Master Teacher (fifth grade)
bullets: Taught multiple grade levels—including a 1/2 combination and extended assignments in fifth and sixth grades—while serving as fifth-grade Master Teacher and Grade Level Coordinator and partnering with the resource specialist on inclusion-style instruction.
Served on the RTI team and the Specialized Academic & Behavior Intervention Team (West End SELPA trained); supported schoolwide Conflict Managers training (West End SELPA program).

Coordinated schoolwide science fair programming; participated in School Site Council, district textbook adoption, PQR team training, and collaborative DARE instruction with the Chino Police Department; completed IB Early Primary Years training and certification.

Two-student outcome (privacy-safe): supported two students often labeled as behavior concerns through relationship-first check-ins, barrier reduction, and interest-based engagement; strengthened family partnership and long-horizon trajectories without sharing identifying details.
story_bullets: Two-student outcome (privacy-safe): supported two students often labeled as behavior concerns through relationship-first check-ins, barrier reduction, and interest-based engagement; strengthened family partnership and long-horizon trajectories without sharing identifying details.

experience.title: Sixth Grade Teacher
bullets: Delivered daily sixth-grade instruction with curriculum development responsibilities and academic intervention supports.
Coordinated schoolwide science fair programming and collaborative DARE instruction with the Chino Police Department.

impact_stories.title: Student outcomes and family partnership

star.situation: Two students were frequently regarded as behavior concerns, with low trust in school and uneven support outside of school affecting engagement.
star.task: Improve engagement and belonging while partnering with families in a respectful, sustainable way.
star.action: Used structured daily check-ins to build trust before raising academic demands; reduced participation barriers when signals indicated basic needs gaps; used interest-based strategies to strengthen self-efficacy and school connection.
star.result: Both students showed stronger engagement and participation; caregivers became active participants in school events after a long gap. Years later, both students described the steady support as important to avoiding negative outcomes and building stability.

impact_stories.star_card_word_count_note: Website STAR text is capped at 160 words total (privacy-safe; no names; no disability labels).

star_web.situation: At Anna Borba, two students were often seen as behavior concerns, with low trust in school and uneven support outside of school.
star_web.task: Improve engagement while partnering with families in a steady, respectful way.
star_web.action: Built trust through structured daily check-ins before raising academic demands; reduced participation barriers when basic-needs signals appeared; used interest-based strategies to strengthen confidence and connection.
star_web.result: Engagement and participation strengthened; caregivers began participating in school events after a long gap. Years later, both students described the consistent support as important to avoiding negative outcomes and building stability.

impact_stories.title: From reading avoidance to elective novel study

star.situation: A student arrived with strong reading avoidance; a caregiver communicated low expectations that the student would become a reader.
star.task: Rebuild confidence without turning reading into a high-stakes performance task on day one.
star.action: Prioritized scaffolded wins, visible early success, and a consistent “you can do this” stance across two school years rather than a remediation-only tone.
star.result: The student moved from avoidance to genuine engagement and self-directed reading interest; later enrolled in high school novel-study electives and credited the earlier confidence foundation as the reason they were willing to try.

impact_stories.star_card_word_count_note: Website STAR text is capped at 160 words total (privacy-safe; no names; no disability labels).

star_web.situation: In junior high English, a student arrived with strong reading avoidance, and a caregiver communicated low expectations that the student would become a reader.
star_web.task: Rebuild confidence without making early reading feel like a high-stakes performance task.
star_web.action: Used scaffolded wins and visible early success across two school years, with a consistent strengths-based stance rather than a remediation-only tone.
star_web.result: The student moved from avoidance toward engagement and self-directed reading interest; in high school, the student chose novel-study electives and credited the earlier confidence foundation as the reason they were willing to try.
